texts.full_text: the use of LVEF in identifying patients who have a higher risk of readmission and carry a worse prognosis. As with the NHF audit, this study has also identified key differences in heart failure management and patient outcomes between those managed on COE and cardiology wards. For example, like the NHF audit, a greater percentage of patients admitted to cardiology wards underwent inpatient echocardiography, suggesting poorer access to specialist investigations by COE teams. A lower percentage of patients on COE teams were also discharged on β blockers, as with the NHF data.[@R3] This finding may partly be explained by the fact that chronic obstructive pulmonary disease was more common in the COE group, although this did not reach statistical significance. Another key finding was the significantly increased mortality in those managed on COE wards versus cardiology wards, and although our data have not been adjusted for age, sex, aetiology, symptoms, treatment and investigations, a similar outcome has also been identified in the NHF audit where such adjustments were made. This study has a number of limitations. First, patient outcomes, such as treatment on discharge and echocardiography, may be under-represented through selection bias, as all patients without a discharge medication summary were excluded from the study, and only inpatient echocardiography, the data of which were available, was looked at. Mortality data in this study may also be under-represented due to the small population size. To improve the validity of our study and to look at the influence of social class/geographical differences on heart failure in the elderly, a larger multicentre study would need to be performed. In summary, this study has shown that investigation and treatment for heart failure in the elderly can be improved, that readmission rates and mortality remain high and that access to specialist cardiology input is associated with better outcomes in terms of treatment and mortality. texts.full_text: m species often cause ear and stalk rot on maize. In addition, a new species, F. temperatum, recently described in Belgium (3), was also identified. F. temperatum is within the Gibberella fujikuroi species complex and is morphologically and phylogenetically closely related to F. subglutinans (2,3). Similar to previous studies (3), our isolates were characterized based on the presence of white cottony mycelium, becoming pinkish white. Conidiophores were erect, branched, and terminating in 1 to 3 phialides. Microconidia were abundant, hyaline, 0 to 2 septa; ellipsoidal to oval, produced singly or in false heads, and on monophialides, intercalary phialides, and polyphialides. Microconidia were not produced in chains. No chlamydospores were observed (3). Macroconidia in carnation leaf agar medium (2) were hyaline, 3 to 6 septate, mostly 4, falcate, with a distinct foot-like basal cell (2,3). DNA was amplified With primers ITS1/ITS4 and EF1/EF2 (3). Partial sequences of gene EF-1α showed 100% homology with F. temperatum (3) (GenBank Accession Nos. HM067687 and HM067688). DNA sequences of EF-1α gene and ITS region obtained were deposited in GenBank (KC179824, KC179825, KC179826, and KC179827). Pathogenicity of one representative isolate was confirmed using a soil inoculation method adapted from Scauflaire et al., 2012 (4). F. temperatum isolate was cultured on sterile wheat grains. Colonized wheat grains (10 g) were mixed with sterilized sand in 10 cm diameter pots. Ten kernels per pot were surface disinfected in 2% sodium hypochlorite for 10 min, rinsed with sterilized water, drained (4), placed on the soil surface, and covered with a 2 cm layer of sterilized sand. Five pots were inoculated and five uninoculated controls were included. Pots were maintained at 22 to 24°C and 80% humidity for 30 days. Seedling malformations, chlorosis, shoot reduction, and stalk rot were observed on maize growing in inoculated soil and not from controls. F. temperatum was reisolated from the inoculated seedlings but not from the controls. References: (1) B. J. Bush et al. Phytopathology 94:88, 2003. (2) j. F.

texts.full_text: ish: "If We Were Together") is a song by Puerto Rican rapper Bad Bunny. The song was released through Rimas Entertainment on December 15, 2018. The nostalgic Spanish-trap song reminisces about a former lover. Composition The song "Si Estuviésemos Juntos" is a Latin trap song and it has a tempo of 86 BPM. Music video The video was released on February 14, 2019 and it currently has more than 45 million views. The nostalgic Spanish-traplyrics revolve around Bad's protagonist reminiscing about his ex-lover after they parted ways due to an argument the previous night (the lyrics imply she might have left him and gone back home). Throughout "Si Estuviésemos Juntos", we get glimmers of what it was like being with her: dancing in bed, laughing together until their hearts hurt. There are also some lyrical references to a love-hate relationship they had. This is the first single from his album I Like It (2019). "Si Estuviésemos Juntos" has been certified Platinum for sales of 400k units by ASURIA in Colombia, where Bad Bunny grew up and spent part time with her. The song's video won Best Music Video award at the Premios Billboard Latin American & Ibero-America awards. Lyrics: Verse: Me alegró Con el sonido de su zancada. F-Fluorothymidine-positron emission tomography with CT fusion ([F]FLT-PET/CT) off[F]-fluoro-thymidin (also known as [18F] fluorothymidine or FTD, and commercially available under the name Fluciclovine; trade names: Axumin® for oncological applications in PET imaging), is a radiotracer used primarily to evaluate cellular proliferation. It's particularly useful because it gets incorporated into DNA during cell division. [F]-fluorothymidin-PET/CT ([F]FLT-PET/CTX) has emerged as an innovative tool, especially valuable when assessing bone marrow function in patients experiencing treatment-induced cytopenias—a condition where the number of blood cells is reduced due to therapy. texts.full_text: without restriction. All relevant data are within the paper and its Supporting Information files. Introduction {#s1} ============ The importance of fleas in human and animal health is largely related to their ability to transmit agents of infectious diseases [@pntd.0003152-Bitam1]. The transmission of these zoonotic agents to human occurs mainly through bites or inoculation of feces intothe skin during the handling of infested animals, making fleas significant vectors in both urban and rural settings [@pntd.0003152-DantasTori1]. Flea-borne diseases include plague (caused by ), murine typhus (), and cat scratch disease () among others [@pntd.0003152-Bitam1; @pntd.0003152-Gupta1]. The risk of these diseases is particularly high in areas with poor sanitation, overcrowding, and close contact between humans and domestic or peridomestic animals such as rats, cats, and dogs [@pntd.0003152-Davies1]. Understanding the ecology, distribution, and behavior of fleas is crucial for developing effective control strategies to reduce disease transmission. Factors influencing flea populations include climate, host availability, and human activities that can alter ecosystems and increase exposure risks [@pntd.0003152-Hinnebusch1]. This study aims to investigate the role of specificenvironmental factorsin the prevalence and diversity of fleas and their associated pathogens, providing insights into potential intervention points for public health interventions. In this context, we conducted a comprehensive survey across multiple geographic regions with varying ecological conditions. Our objectives were to identify flea species present, assess the infection rates with zoonotic agents,and analyze how environmental factors influence these

The anti-malarial artemisinin (Art) is a pure sesquiterpene lactone extracted from the Chinese herb Artemisia annua (qinghao, sweet wormwood). In thepresent study, we investigated the effect of artemisinin on the expression of pro-inflammatory cytokines in PMA-induced THP-1 monocytes, a human monocytic cell line often used to model inflammatory responses. Our results showed that artemisinin significantly inhibited the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, such as TNF-α, IL-1β, and IL-6, by interfering with the NF-κB canonical signaling pathway. texts.full_text: Mystery and the Peripatetic", with other poets and creatives, mostly from the state. In the closing session Dr. David Rothman spoke of Belle Turnbull, a poet of enormous talent and promise who moved to Breckenridge early in her career, and thus, working away from the centers of the poetry establishment on the coasts, never got her just due, despite stellar reviews of her work. She is now nearly forgotten, even by Colorado poets, and based on the samples of her work offered by Rothman, her obscurity is a true literary crime. I thought, said Mr. Probus, there was time, Time by the dipperful, time lipping, flowing Out of some plenteous spring where I'd be going With my bright dipper, frosting it with rime, Hoarding no more than God would a dime, Slipping time over my palate, careless blowing Drops off my moustache, wasting it well knowing There would be more, more always, soft and prime. –from "At That Point Mr. Probus: Time as a Wellspring" by Belle Turnbull, for which she won the 1938 Harriet Monroe Memorial Prize from Poetry Magazine, alongside Dylan Thomas, no less. (Belle Turnbull in 1926)Born in Hamilton, New York on December 16, 1894, Belle Turnbull showed early promise as a poet, publishing her first poem at the age of 16. She attended Smith College, where she studied under renowned poets such as Mary Colum and John Gould Fletcher. After graduating in 1916, she moved to Breckenridge, Colorado, where she spent much of her career writing and teaching poetry. Turnbull's work is characterized by its vivid imagery, deep emotional resonance, and exploration of themes such as time, nature, and the human condition. Her poems often reflect her love for the Colorado landscape and its rugged beauty. Despite her talent and critical acclaim, Turnbull remained largely unknown outside of Colorado during

texts.full_text: e PPACA? These are important questions that have been subject to legal challenges since 2010. The ACA (Affordable Care Act) has faced significant opposition, particularly among conservative lawmakers and organizations. The individual mandate—a key component of the law requiring individuals with certain incomes above a threshold or no health insurance at all will pay an additional tax for not having coverage. Employer mandates refer to provisions that require employers who meet specific size thresholds (100 employees) provide their workers, full-time equivalent status and affordable employer-sponsored plans. Failure in doing so can result punitive taxes. The challenge around state Medicaid expansion centers on states' rights argument—specifically the mandate requiring each individual American citizens or legal residents have access to health insurance through government-exchange markets. Standing refers more generally whether a person, business entity (including governments and other entities), has an actual case about which they can request court action. The principle of standing is designed preventively ensure that individuals only are entitled challenge laws when those challenges involve concrete injury or real impact. The legal status remains in flux; the U.S Court systems continue to grapple with these issues on various levels, from individual states up through district courts all way upti

texts.full_text: injury (mTBI) can have a negative impact on quality of life, functional abilities, and rehabilitative progress. In this study, we used a range of dynamic and static objective laboratory and clinical measurements of accommodation to assess 12 adult patients (ages 18-40 years) with mTBI. The results were compared with either 10 control subjects with no visual impairment or normative literature values where available. Regarding the dynamic parameteThe dynamic parameter of accommodation, namely the accommodation amplitude, was significantly impaired in the mTBI group compared to the control group. The mTBI group exhibited a mean accommodation amplitude of 3.3 diopters, which was significantly lower than the control group's mean of 4.4 diopters. This impaired accommodation amplitude in mTBI patients may contribute to the reported symptoms of blurred vision, eye strain, and difficulties in focusing on near and far objects. In terms of sTatic accommodation, the mTBI group demonstrated a near point of accommodation (NPA) of 4.6 cm, on average, which was also significantly lower than the control group's average of 6.4 cm. A near point of accommodation of 4.6 cm indicates a level of accommodation impairment, as the normal range for NPA is 6 cm to 10 cm. texts.full_text: You can find the original article HERE. Mortgages come in two primary forms, fixed rate and adjustable rate, with some hybrid combinations and multiple derivatives of each. A basic understanding of interest rates and the economic influences that determine the future course of interest rates can help consumers make financially sound mortgage decisions, such as making the choice between a fixed-rate mortgage or adjustable-rate mortgage (ARM) or deciding whetherto buy now or wait for better rates. Interest rates are one of the most important factors affecting the housing market. When rates are low, it becomes more affordable for consumers to borrow money to purchase homes. This generally leads to an increase in demand for homes and can drive up home prices. Conversely, when interest rates rise, the cost of borrowing increases, which can reduce demand for homes and lead to lower home prices. The Federal Reserve (the Fed), which is the central bank of the United States, plays a significant role in determining interest rates through its monetary policy. The Fed influences interest rates primarily through its control over the federal funds rate, which is the interest rate at which depository institutions lend reserve balances to other depository institutions overnight. When the Fed wants to stimulate economic growth, it typically lowers the federal funds rate target. Lowering the federal funds rate makes borrowing cheaper for consumers and businesses, which can lead to increased spending and investment. This can boost economic activity, including in the housing market, by making mortgages more affordable and encouraging homebuying. On the other hand, when the Fed wants to cool down an overheating economy or combat inflation, it may raise the federal funds rate target. Raising interest rates makes borrowing more expensive for consumers and businesses, which can slow down spending and investment. This can reduce economic activity, including in the housing market, by making mortgages more expensive and discouraging homebuying. texts.full_text: eam Alcohol and Drug Abuse Prevention Team (ADAPT) ADAPT is dedicated to addressing the issues of alcohol and other drug abuse in our community through education, intervention, and support services. Our mission is to promote healthy lifestyles and prevent substance abuse among individuals of all ages. We offer a variety of programs and resources designed to help individuals and families affected by alcohol and drug abuse. These include: 1. Education and Awareness Campaigns: We conduct educational campaigns in schools, workplaces, and community settings to raise awareness about the dangers of alcohol and drug abuse. Our goal is to provide information on how to recognize signs of substance use disorders and where to seek help. 2. Intervention Services: For those struggling with addiction, we offer intervention services that can help them take the first steps towards recovery. This includes assessments, counseling, and referrals to treatment centers. 3. Support Services: We provide ongoing support for individuals in recovery through support groups, counseling sessions, and other resources. Our aim is to create a supportive environment where individuals can share their experiences and receive encouragement as they work towards a healthier lifestyle. 4. Community Outreach: ADAPT actively engages with the community to promote healthy choices and prevent substance abuse. We collaborate with local organizations, law enforcement agencies, and healthcare providers to address alcohol and drug issues at the grassroots level. texts.full_text: t and therapeutic advice for patients with cognitive disorders. Detailed information about the DOC-PG can be found elsewhere [5]. One GNP has been appointed within the DOC-PG team to communicate the advice from the DOC-PG to all the GPs who referred patients to the DOC-PG. After screening and assessment of the patient by the DOC-PG team, the results of the diagnostic process are discussed with the patient and his or her informal caregiver by the geriatric psychiatrist and the GNP (this discussion also took place in the reference sample with the exception that no GNP was present). Immediately after this disclosure, the GNP contacted the GP by telephone and discussed the diagnosis and advice given by the DOC-PG team; together, they allocated the tasks and formulated a plan of action. The GNP monitored this plan of action and registered the care-trajectory in consultation with the GP. In addition, the informal caregiver could contact the GNP whenever necessary. In summation, the GNP functioned as a mediator between the DOC-PG facility and the GP and, in addition, was a contact person for informal caregivers and patients. In the reference sample, the GPs only received a summary of the assessments, diagnosis and recommendations for treatment and management by written communication. Measures GP level For both cohorts, compliance with advice formulated by the DOC-PG team was checked by means of a concordance checklist, which was sent to each GP who referred a patient to the DOC-PG during the inclusion period. The concordance checklist listed all advice or recommendations from the multidisciplinary team at the patient level from July 2002 to August 2004 (reference sample), and from March 2010 to September 2011 (GNP group). The GP was requested to indicate whether advice from the DOC-PG team had been followed or not (yes/no). texts.full_text: built to offer a to z solution on IPv4 addresses, and how it relates to TCP/IP and Internet. There are times when you need to identify where your web visitors are coming from. You might have an ecommerce website, and would like to know where your potential customers are, pre-populate country code on forms, display different language and reduce credit card fraud based on geographic location. Or, you might want to fight against illegal spammers and hackers, and would like to locate source of a problem. The major search engines such as Google, Yahoo and MSN/Bing are becoming ever more sophisticated and smart in their search algorithms, and incorporate historical and IP address data, it is now even more important than ever to ensure that you take the necessary precautions when designing a network of lwebsites. It can be very frustrating for your visitors if they land on an irrelevant website due to geographic location. IP Geolocation Check-my-ip.net has built one simple tool called "What Is My IP Address", simply visit whatismyipaddress.com from any Computer, and it will show you the ip address currently assigned by ISP. This is a very useful tools for troubleshooting routing and communications problem in any TCP/IP network because knowing your external or public IPV4 mapping can help to resolve problems with communication between computers over internet. texts.full_text: year-old headbanger in 1990. They were tight and energetic and the new material sounded good to my ears. If I had one complaint it would be that they didn't play long enough! Testament took the stage around 8:45 and played a set list that was a mix of their classic stuff and new material from their most recent release, Dark Roots of Earth. Chuck Billy's vocals were on point and the band sounded great. They played a few songs off of my all-time favorite Testament album, The New Order (1988), including "Over the Wall" and "Into the Pit." The crowd went nuts for those songs and I was headbanging along with them. The new material was also very well received by the crowd. After a brief intermission, Anthrax hit the stage around 10:30. I've seen Anthrax twice before, once in 2003 and again in 2005, but this was by far the best performance I've seen from them. They opened with "Worship Music," the title track off of their new album, and played a mix of old and new material. Scott Ian's guitar work was outstanding and Joey Belladonna's vocals were powerful and on key throughout the entire set. The show was a great value for the money and all three bands put on an excellent performance. I left the show feeling very satisfi

Laparoscopic aspiration resulted in the collection of significantly more clear FF compared to the transvaginal procedure (P = 0.001). Uncontaminated fluid depicted a single mean absorbance at 458.0 nm. Blood contaminated FF could be identified by spectrophotometry and revealed three peaks at 418.12, 540.13, and 575.32 nm, respectively, according to the degree of blood contamination. Follicular fluid diluted with Earles Balanced Salt Solution (EBSS) displayed an additional mean peak at 561.4 nm. Potassium, glucose, and all of the above mentioned enzymes revealed significantly higher levels in blood contaminated FF (P < 0.05). From this study, it is concluded that blood contamination and dilution with culture medium influence the biochemical composition as well as the absorbance spectrum of follicular fluids. texts.full_text: of the villa, the cool and spacious bedrooms are luxuriously appointed with high-grade finishes and premium materials. All bedrooms are en-suite with neutral mosaic tiles, sleek rainfall showers and inviting cubist baths. The top floor master suite has floor-to-ceiling windows on two sides of the room looking out to the incredible vistas beyond. Interior The chic and spacious interior is intelligently laid out, with integrated living, dining and kitchen areas giving a distinctly enjoyable and sociable vibe. The kitchen is of sleek and seamless design and equipped to perfection for aspiring gourmands. The stylish and comfortable lounge area looking out over the views is the perfect space to escape the heat of the day and relax in the blissful coolness of the villa. Throughout the property, the ambience is one of pure calmness combined with effortless, uncluttered style. Exterior The frontline feature on the grounds of Can Beso is the villa’s vast and decadent infinity pool with integrated Jacuzzi, inviting those LA pool party vibes. Luxurious seating and suspended day beds offer stylish options for lounging while soaking up the views, that are uninterrupted by the glass walled perimeter. A large outside dining space is beneath the shaded terrace. The grounds of Can Beso are completely walled with an automatic gate and secure entry system. There is a two-berth garage with direct villa access and further parking
